observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Management take the following courses of action:

a.	Require the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property cards and property ledger cards, respectively, and update them periodically to aid the conduct of physical count of PPE.





b. 	Conduct a complete physical count of all PPE excluding CIP annually and prepare the required RPCPPE.





c. 	Thereafter, reconcile the physical count reports with the balances of PPE accounts per books to establish the correct balances that should be presented in the financial statements.


d. Avail of the opportunity provided by the COA for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances by following the procedures and guidelines set in COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the submission of all the DVs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with the timelines and requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant include in the Notes to FSs the required disclosures on the reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the SCBAA and in the SFPerf for the year then ended. Its format be based on Annex G of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We recommended the following:

a. The Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own LGUs to adopt their procedures and best practices.



b. Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We recommended the following:

a. 	The Municipal Mayor organize the Municipality's Appraisal Committee which will provide for the fair values of the received PPE at the time of acquisition.

b. 	The Municipal Accountant, based on the information and documents provided by the heads of the receiving municipal department/office and the fair values of the received PPE provided by the Appraisal Committee, record the received assets and its related revenues in the Municipality's books of accounts by debiting the Specific PPE accounts and crediting the Grants and Donations in Kind account with account number 4-04-02-020. Specifically, for the unrecorded receipt of donations in kind in CY 2022, the Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entry:

Account Name              Account Number  Debit  Credit
Specific PPE                                                xxx
Prior Period Adjustment   3-01-01-020                   xxx

To record PPE received as donations from other government agencies in CY 2022.

We recommended that the Management document and submit to the Commission on Audit the request for authority to write-off the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that Management should exert more efforts to locate the supporting documents of this loan receivable account. These documents will provide information to support the claims of the LGU to demand payments. And should said efforts fail, the LGU shall request COA for the condonation/write-off of the receivables accounts subject to prescribed guidelines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the drivers and other concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Audit Team every month for audit examination and evaluation.

3. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the accumulated depreciation of the road networks, net of the cost of road lots, prepare the necessary corrections, and provide the required disclosures in the notes in accordance with IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.
4. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to regularly review the Local Road Network Ledger Card, request updated records from the Municipal Engineering Office for its infrastructure projects, and continuously provide depreciation for each depreciable component of the local road network system.
5. We further recommended and Management agreed to create an Inventory Committee to conduct an actual physical count of the local road network system and report the results thereof in the RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting unit not later than January 31 of each year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records, and the necessary adjusting entries shall be prepared before the statement date.

The Municipal Treasurer then update the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card based on the latest/updated assessment roll provided by the Municipal Assessor and provide the Municipal Accountant the computation of tax collectibles for the year;
The Municipal Accountant draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the information provided by the Municipal Treasurer on the amount due and collectible for the year, using the following pro-forma entry; and

4.7	We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer:
Establish physical controls over the release of accountable forms such as keeping the forms in a vault/receptacle/cabinet with lock and use of the CCTV;
Regularly review the correctness and completeness of reporting of accountable forms as appearing in the RCDs and RAAFs of the accountable officers and reconcile them with the accountable forms record book; and
Ensure that only sufficient quantities of accountable forms are released to accountable officers based on their actual needs and no releases be made if previous collections remain unremitted.

5.7	We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed on the following:
The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) set up and maintain a GAD database or, in coordination with the Information Technology Officer II, incorporate it to its existing IComSys database which should contain complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2024-01;
Management provide budgetary and technical support to the MPDO in the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database; and
The GAD Focal Point System – Technical Working Group use sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information provided by the GAD database in identifying gender issues and gaps and their related projects and activities for inclusion in the gender-responsive GPB.

1.5. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Office Order
reconstituting the Inventory Committee with adequate members, including at
least one member from each of the Accounting and Property Divisions/Units,
and temporarily relieve them of all their regular duties until the physical
inventory process is complete.

1.6. We further recommended that once created/reconstituted, the Inventory
Committee:

1.6.1. Strictly follow the guidelines and procedures for conducting the physical
count of PPE. This includes recognizing PPE items found at the station
and disposing of items that are non-existent or missing. The Accountant
and Property Officer should reconcile their records based on the results
of the actual physical count and make any necessary adjustments;3
1.6.2. Adopt a uniform numbering system for PPE property numbers and
require the Property Unit to update the property stickers according to
the prescribed format and include all the necessary information as
specified in Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020; and
1.6.3. Prepare the PIP for approval by the Municipal Mayor and submit the
same to COA at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of
inventory activities.

2.6. We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive create an
Inventory Committee to focus on the physical count of local roads and enjoin
the Municipal Engineer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer to
strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads
asset management system pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated
November 23, 2015.

2.7. We further recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to provide the
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with a complete description
and segregation of road components for road projects.

2.8. We furthermore recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
disclose the total road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements.

3.4. We recommended that the:

3.4.1. Local Chief Executive reconstitute the Inventory Committee to include
members who can be relieved of their regular duties to devote full time
to conducting the physical inventory taking until the same is completed;
and
3.4.2. Inventory Committee prepare and submit a copy of the inventory reports
to the Auditor to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS
Manual for LGUs;

3.5. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer agreed to maintain the supplies ledger cards and stock cards,
respectively.

3.6. We furthermore recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly
follow the Perpetual Inventory Method for recording purchases of supplies and
materials as required in the NGAS Manual.

3.7. During the exit conference, Management acknowledged that this had been a
longstanding deficiency of the Municipality. To facilitate the issuance of an
Office Order reconstituting the Inventory Committee, the department heads
present agreed to draft the proposed composition of the committee for the
Municipal Mayor’s approval.

4.11. We recommended and the Municipal Agriculturist agreed to:

4.11.1. Conduct an inventory of breeding stocks as of the end of each year and
thereafter render a report including the fair market value of these items
to the Municipal Accountant, for proper recording in the books of the
LGU; and
4.11.2. Keep a systematic record of the Municipality’s breeding stocks,
reporting births, deaths, and disposal, and in case of death and disposal,
facilitate request for relief of accountability in accordance with COA
Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, to appropriately drop the
amount from the books.

4.12. We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose the
composition of the Biological Assets account in the Notes to Financial Statements
in accordance with IPSAS 27 to provide relevant information that may affect
the fairness of the presentation of the financial statements.

5.9. We recommended and the LDRRMO agreed to:

5.9.1. Include PPAs that are chargeable against the prior years’ unexpended
LDRRMF in the annual LDRRMFIP of the current year, in accordance
with the format prescribed under Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-
002; and
5.9.2. Establish an efficient review and monitoring system to ensure that the
LDRRMFIP complies with the requirements outlined in COA Circular
No. 2012-002.

6.5. We recommended and Management agreed to:
6.5.1. Desist from charging the cost of medicines and animal bite vaccines for
the RHU and infirmary and honoraria for volunteer rescue responders
to the LDRRMF;
6.5.2. Reimburse the LDRRMF from the General Fund for the cost of
honoraria for volunteer rescue responders and medicines and animal bite
vaccines charged against the Quick-Response Fund; and
6.5.3. Adhere strictly to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No.
2012-73 dated April 17, 2012, and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No.
2013-1 dated March 25, 2013, on the utilization of the LDRRMF.

Recommendations:
We recommended the Local Chief Executive implement the following remedial measures in compliance with IPSAS No. 17 and COA Circular No. 92-386:
Instruct the personnel in-charge to exhaust all possible means to facilitate the legal processing of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificates of Title (TCT) in the name of the Barangay, and seek budgetary allocations for the pertinent costs thereof; and
Provide copies of TCTs to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the establishment of a subsidiary list.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:
The Municipal Accountant to adjust the LGU’s books of accounts to accurately reflect the unexpended balances of the Quick Response Fund (QRF) and DRRM- MOOE for CY 2024 under the Special Trust Liability – LDRRMF, and ensure that all unutilized DRRM funds from previous years (CY 2020-2023) are recognized as part of the Special Trust Liability in accordance to COA Circular No. 2012-002, and
The Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the transfer of the insufficient unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the trust fund account and to ensure the timely transfer of unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE in future periods.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Implement Strict Remittance Schedules by enforcing a policy requiring collecting officers to remit collections to the Municipal Treasurer within two days or as soon as the collections reach the ₱10,000.00 threshold.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit and deposit all collections intact and on a daily basis, or in accordance with the frequency prescribed by relevant regulations to maintain proper control and accountability over public funds and minimize the risk of misappropriation or loss.
Implement stricter internal controls over the collection and deposit processes. This includes the regular monitoring and review of procedures to ensure adherence to the provisions of the DOF-BLGF Local Treasury Operations Manual, COA Circular No. 92-382 and the Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units (GAM for LGUs) to ensure the timely and intact deposit of collections and safeguard public funds from potential misuse.
Establish a systematic approach to regularly monitor and review collection and deposit procedures to ensure compliance with established guidelines to identify and address any weaknesses or irregularities in the process and ensure the integrity and authenticity of financial transactions.
Implement accountability measures for officers who fail to comply with the established rules, including disciplinary actions and reporting to higher authorities.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:
The Municipal Treasurer to direct all Revenue Collectors to remit all their collections promptly and review all their Reports of Collections and Deposits to ensure that all collections and issued and unissued accountable forms are fully accounted for to prevent loss or misuse of collections. The Treasurer shall:
Verify the Report of Collections and Deposits, particularly examining the Statement of Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues, and ending balances on hand. Conduct a physical count of accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collector and check the same against new balances on the hand column; and
Conduct a physical count of accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collector and check the same against new balances on hand. c. Issue accountable forms only to those accountable officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections in compliance with the provisions of Section 30 of the Manual on NGAS Volume I.
Issue accountable forms only to those accountable officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections in compliance with the provisions of Section 30 of the Manual on NGAS Volume I.
All revenue collectors are to prepare their respective RAAF, and the Municipal Treasurer is to prepare the Consolidated RAAF and submit these to our office by the 5th day of the ensuing month.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:
Strictly implement NBC No. 563 by limiting payment of registration fees to
₱2,000.00 a day;
Exercise due prudence in the granting of cash advances and reimbursement of expenses such that government funds are protected and conserved to prevent excessive expenditures as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012,
To require concerned officials and employees to refund any excess grant of cash advances and reimbursements unless reasonable justification was provided.
Require all officials and employees to seek prior approval for attending conventions, seminars, and similar gatherings, ensuring that the expenses are within the allowable limits.
Implement a monitoring system to track and review all expenses related to registration fees for non-training gatherings to prevent future occurrences of excessive spending.

Recommendations:
To ensure the timely submission of Financial Transaction Documents and Reports, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to immediately submit the outstanding financial transaction documents and reports for December 2024 and regularly prepare and promptly submit monthly reports within the prescribed period.
Implement a more efficient process for reviewing the completeness of attachments to ensure that complete documentation is secured before payment, allowing only the act of filing at month-end.
Conduct a study to determine the optimal number of employees needed at the Municipal Accounting Office to handle the volume of transactions and prevent delays.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:
Prioritize the formal appointment of a qualified Municipal Budget Officer, ensuring that the financial management and budgeting processes are effectively overseen. The acting MBO should be officially appointed the intended functionality of the Municipal Budget Office and ensure compliance with Sections 443 and 475 of R.A. 7160 and Sections 4 and 5 of CSC Memorandum
Circular No. 12, Series of 2022;
Instruct the Human Resource Management Officer to publish the vacancy for the position promptly and
Strictly adhere to the legal requirements for creating and appointing mandatory positions as specified in the relevant laws, such as the Local Government Code and CSC Memorandum Circular No. 12, Series of 2022, and guidelines.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management:
Monitor and expedite the implementation of the remaining projects under the 20% LDF to ensure the timely delivery of essential services and maximize the socio- economic benefits for its constituents.
Convene the Local Development Council to revisit the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan and the 20% Local Development Fund Plan to determine and prioritize the implementation of feasible socio-economic programs, projects, and activities that will benefit the general welfare of its constituents, following the provisions of Section 4 of DBM-DOF-DILG Circular No. 2020-01 and RA No. 7160.
Provide and attend training sessions and workshops on procurement law, guidelines, and best practices procurement officers, finance personnel, and project managers to comply with regulations, and process transactions in a timely manner, reducing costly mistakes and delays.
Create a cross-functional working group composed of representatives from key municipal offices to oversee the implementation of projects under the 20% LDF to ensure that procurement and disbursement processes involve multiple
offices, are in constant communication to prevent bottlenecks and ensures that proper documentation is delivered on time, aligning all parties on project priorities and timelines.

Recommendations:
We recommend that Management require the Municipal Engineer to:
Submit immediately the lacking documents needed for the technical inspection for timely review and evaluation and inspection by the COA Technical Audit Group; and
Require the personnel in charge of submitting contracts and their supporting documents to maintain a checklist to ensure the completeness and propriety of all required supporting documents for contracts submitted to COA for review, in accordance with COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, as amended by COA Circular No. 2023-004 dated June 14, 2023, and COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Management, specifically:

a.	The Local Finance Committee, in its proposal for provision of estimated revenues to be authorized in the Municipality’s Annual Budget, project income that are realistic and collectible and bare estimates from sound forecasting system.

b.	The Municipal Accountant accomplish the Local Budget Accountability Form No. 1 or the Quarterly Report of Income based on the actual collection of the Acting Municipal Treasurer to keep track of the status of revenue generation.

c.	The Municipal Mayor, together with the Acting Municipal Treasurer, conduct periodic review of the collection performance of all revenue items in the budget to ascertain that targets are attained so as to provide assurance that PPAs of the Municipality are all implemented as planned because estimated revenues are collected.

We recommended the following:

a. The Municipal Budget Officer see to it that the computation of the allowable CF be strictly based on the budget of the Municipality’s POP only.

b. The Municipal Accountant ensure that the amount of confidential expenses will not exceed the authorized funds provided for the CF.

We recommended that, prospectively, Management see to it that projects to be implemented from grants, donations and assistance of private and other government organizations which have expirations periods or are time-bounded should be prioritized and implemented timely and in accordance with the agreed terms and conditions and/or guidelines to avoid reversion of the funds.

We recommended the following:

a. The Municipal Accountant prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF using the prescribed format in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate its timely submission.

b. The LDRRMO then submit the report to the Office of the Auditor on or before 15th day after the end of each month for us to review and/or evaluate the fund sources, fund allocations in each thematic area and the expenditures charged against the fund.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain a Cashbook for Cash in Bank for all funds and bank accounts in the required form pursuant to the requirements and instructions provided in the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the submission of the duly accomplished Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation on the audit observations and recommendations embodied in the AAR. All columns in the form be properly accomplished including the detailed action plan, person/department responsible, target implementation date, and status of implementation.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor and all other Municipal officials and employees strictly comply with the requirements and mechanics prescribed under Presidential EO No. 77 in their claims for reimbursement for official travels. Specifically:

All claims for official travel should not exceed the rates authorized under Presidential EO No. 77.

Expenses of the Municipal Mayor other than travelling expenses, during the duration of the travel, be charged to other line-item expense budgets, subject to availability of funds and the limits set in Section 325 (h) of RA No. 7160.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Management take the following courses of action:


a.	Require the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property cards and property ledger cards respectively and update them periodically to aid the conduct of physical count of PPE.




b.	Conduct a complete physical count of all PPE excluding CIP annually and prepare the required reports thereof.




c.	Thereafter, reconcile the physical count reports with the balances of PPE accounts per books to establish the correct balances that should be presented in the FSs.




d. Avail of the opportunity provided by the COA for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances by following the procedures and guidelines set in COA Circular 2020-006.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant include in the Notes to FSs the required disclosures on the reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the SCBAA and in the SFPerf for the year then ended. Its format be based on Annex G of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We recommended the following:

a.	The Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to improve the efficiency of collection of real property taxes. Avail of all collection remedies applicable in the current circumstance of the Municipality.

b.	The Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own LGUs to adopt their procedures and best practices.




c.	Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We recommended the following:

a. 	The Municipal Mayor and the heads of the receiving municipal department/office inform the Municipal Accountant whenever they receive donations in kind from other government agencies and private individuals so that the PPE received and their related revenues be properly recognized, measured and recorded in the Municipality's books of accounts.

b. 	The Municipal Mayor organize the Municipality's Appraisal Committee which will provide for the fair values of the received PPE at the time of acquisition.




c. 	The Municipal Accountant, based on the information and documents provided by the heads of the receiving municipal department/office and the fair values of the received PPE provided by the Appraisal Committee, record the received assets and its related revenues in the Municipality's books of accounts by debiting the Specific PPE accounts and crediting the Grants and Donations in Kind account with account number 4-04-02-020. Specifically, for the unrecorded receipt of donations in kind in CY 2022, the Municipal Accountant prepare the following journal entry:

Account Name              Account Number  Debit  Credit
Specific PPE                                                xxx
Prior Period Adjustment   3-01-01-020                   xxx

To record PPE received as donations from other government agencies in CY 2022.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor and all other Municipal officials and employees strictly comply with the requirements and mechanics prescribed under Presidential EO No. 77 in their claims for reimbursement for official travels. Specifically:

Official local travels of the Municipal Mayor be covered with duly approved travel orders/authorities to travel issued by the Provincial Governor.

All claims for official travel should not exceed the rates authorized under Presidential EO No. 77.




Accommodation claims in excess of the accommodation component of the DTE, but not to exceed 100% of such component, be supported with a certification by the Head of the Agency that those expenses were absolutely necessary in the performance of their assignments.

Expenses of the Municipal Mayor other than travelling expenses, during the duration of the travel, be charged to other line-item expense budgets, subject to availability of funds and the limits set in Section 325 (h) of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160.

We recommended that the Management document and submit to the Commission on Audit the request for authority to write-off the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that Management should exert more efforts to locate the supporting documents of this loan receivable account. These documents will provide information to support the claims of the LGU to demand payments. And should said efforts fail, the LGU shall request COA for the condonation/write-off of the receivables accounts subject to prescribed guidelines.

observations.recommendation:
1.16  We reiterated our recommendations that the Management take the
following courses of action:
a.	Require the Property Officer and the Municipal Accountant to maintain property cards and property ledger cards, respectively, and update them periodically to aid the conduct of physical count of PPE.
b.	Conduct a complete physical count of all PPE excluding CIP annually and prepare the required RPCPPE.
c.	Thereafter, reconcile the physical count reports with the balances of PPE accounts per books to establish the correct balances that should be presented in the financial statements.
d. Avail of the opportunity provided by the COA for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances by following the procedures and guidelines set in COA Circular 2020-006.

2.6    We recommended that the Management, specifically:
The Local Finance Committee, in its proposal for provision of estimated revenues to be authorized in the Municipality’s Annual Budget, project income that are realistic and collectible and bare estimates from sound forecasting system.
The Municipal Accountant accomplish the Local Budget Accountability Form No. 1 or the Quarterly Report of Income based on the actual collection of the Acting Municipal Treasurer to keep track of the status of revenue generation.
The Municipal Mayor, together with the Acting Municipal Treasurer, conduct periodic review of the collection performance of all revenue items in the budget to ascertain that targets are attained so as to provide assurance that PPAs of the Municipality are all implemented as planned because estimated revenues are collected.

3.9   We recommended the following:
The Municipal Budget Officer see to it that the computation of the allowable CF be strictly based on the budget of the Municipality’s POP only.
The Municipal Accountant ensure that the amount of confidential expenses will not exceed the authorized funds provided for the CF.

4.9   We recommended that, prospectively, Management see to it that projects to be implemented from grants, donations and assistance of private and other government organizations which have expirations periods or are time-bounded should be prioritized and implemented timely and in accordance with the agreed terms and conditions and/or guidelines to avoid reversion of the funds.

5.5   We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant prepare the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF using the prescribed format in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate its timely submission.
The LDRRMO then submit the report to the Office of the Auditor on or before 15th day after the end of each month for us to review and/or evaluate the fund sources, fund allocations in each thematic area and the expenditures charged against the fund.

6.5  We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain a Cashbook for Cash in Bank for all funds and bank accounts in the required form pursuant to the requirements and instructions provided in the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.

7.7   We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the submission of all the DVs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with the timelines and requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2009-006.

8.6    We recommended that the Management strictly adhere to the submission of the duly accomplished Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation on the audit observations and recommendations embodied in the AAR. All columns in the form be properly accomplished including the detailed action plan, person/department responsible, target implementation date, and status of implementation.

1.11 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review the records of the affected accounts, determine the correct date of acquisition of the property, and prepare the corresponding adjusting entries, if necessary, to recognize the depreciation for the period in accordance with IPSAS 17 and Section 262 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume 1.

1.12 We likewise recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to check the accuracy of PPE accounts recorded in the Trust Fund books and verify if there are items included in the account but did not meet the capitalization threshold required and prepare the adjusting entry if necessary, in compliance with Section 139, of the GAM for LGUs, Volume 1.

2.16 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record the accumulated depreciation of the additional road networks, net of the cost of road lots, prepare the necessary corrections, and provide the required disclosures in the notes in accordance with IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015.

2.17 We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to regularly review the Local Road Network Ledger Card, request updated records from the Municipal Engineering Office for its infrastructure projects, and continuously provide depreciation for each depreciable component of the local road network system.

2.18 We further recommended and Management agreed to create an Inventory Committee to conduct an actual physical count of the local road network system and report the results thereof in the RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting unit not later than January 31 of each year. The RPCLRN shall be reconciled with the accounting records, and the necessary adjusting entries are prepared before the statement date.

3.10 We recommended and Management agreed to:

3.9.1. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balances of the LDRRM Fund for CY 2023 from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund.

3.9.2. Require the Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of unexpended LDRRMF by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the appropriate accounting entries under General Fund and Trust Fund.

3.9.3. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 21 of R.A. 10121 and Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

4.10 We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions for Shopping under the alternative method of procurement pursuant to Section 52.1 and Annex H of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

4.11 We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to review the supporting documents attached to the Disbursement Vouchers to ensure that the forms are filled out and supplied the needed information.

5.13 We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 305(a) of R.A. No. 7160 and Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445 in the disbursement of government funds and refrain from using public funds without the proper specific authority of law to avoid audit suspension and/or disallowance.

5.14 We further recommended and the accountable official concerned agreed to submit the required supporting documents to the Audit Team for validation and evaluation, otherwise the same will be disallowed in audit.

6.7 We recommended that Management install internal control mechanisms by using the RIS to issue medicines and medical supplies and consolidate the same in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI), which shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant to record the appropriate expense accounts in the books for the supplies consumed.

6.8 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop the practice of directly recording purchases of pharmaceutical products and medical supplies in the related expense accounts and henceforth record purchases and utilization thereof in accordance with Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs to present the expense and inventory accounts fairly in the financial statements.

6.9 Management assured the Audit Team that they would revise the current inventory procedures as soon as the construction of a medicine warehouse is completed this year.

7.8 We recommended that Management repair or replace the locks of the vault and steel cabinets where cash on hand and unused accountable forms may be kept and safeguarded.

7.9 We also recommended that Management provide additional security equipment like a CCTV camera or a security guard as additional safeguards of government funds and property to protect them from possible loss or defalcation in consonance with the provision of Sections 2 and 123 of P.D. No. 1445.

8.12 We recommended that Management revisit the existing policies, guidelines, rules, and regulations on the payment of overtime services to medical personnel in accordance with CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2015 and the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 7305.

8.13 We also recommended that Management review its internal rules and procedures on the rendition of overtime services of their personnel in consonance with the policies and guidelines provided under the aforesaid Joint Circular.

8.14 We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the proper documentary requirements, like the duly accomplished DTR, overtime work program, and regular monthly duty hours, for review by the audit team pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001.

8.15 In addition, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer submit if there are additional documents relative to the augmentation of funds for overtime services.

8.16 Management will inquire or check with the Provincial Government and other LGUs on the payment of overtime pay for hospital and medical specialists.

9.9 We recommended that Management fill up the vacant positions of Local DRRM Officer III and the three assistants to head the LDRRMO with a sense of urgency, in compliance with Item 5.5.3 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.

9.10 Management commented that currently, there are still no qualified personnel available to take up the DRRM positions.

10.12 We recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the existing policies and guidelines for hiring contract of service and job order workers pursuant to CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, dated June 15, 2017.

10.13 We also recommended and Management agreed to review its internal rules and procedures on the rendition of overtime services of their personnel in consonance with the policies and guidelines provided under the aforesaid Joint Circular.

10.14 We further recommended and Management agreed that the concerned COS personnel submit the proper documentary requirements, like the properly accomplished DTR, accomplishment report, and regular monthly duty hours, for review and evaluation by the Audit Team.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Obtain the monthly bank statements in various alternative ways, such as securing them from their authorized government depository bank, requesting a copy of bank snapshots available from Municipal Treasurer’s office, and utilizing the functionalities in the weAccess bank account to view and download payments, collections, and other reports necessary for reconciling items. Subsequently, prepare the corresponding monthly BRS for each account, according to the number of copies required in compliance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.
Submit monthly the BRS to COA not later than 15 working days after the following month in accordance with Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require:
The Municipal Treasurer maintain and update its duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year and furnish the same to Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year.
The Municipal Accountant to establish at the beginning of the year the RPT/SET Receivables based from the RPTAR of the Municipal Treasurer.
The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances per master list of RPT delinquencies with that of the RPT and SET Receivable account balances per books of accounts to come up with a more accurate and proper balance of RPT and SET Receivable accounts.
The Municipal Assessor to recover, establish and maintain the assessment roll of other barangays for effective assessment and collection of RPT.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Establish an Inventory Committee dedicated only to conduct physical count of Local Road Network starting with the road network system recorded in the books. In the conduct of physical count, determine each component of the local roads, its costs or fair market value (if without identified costs) and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network.
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer, before conducting the physical count,  reconcile their records to ensure that the road network system recorded in the books is reflected in the Inventory of Road Network that shall be maintained by the Municipal Treasurer/MGSO;
Direct the Municipal Assessor to provide to the Municipal Accountant copies of TDs of Road Lots under the name of the LGU tor recording in the books.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer/MGSO, to prepare, maintain and regularly update the Local Road Network Property Card, in accordance with the prescribed format per COA Circular No. 2015-008.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the LRNLC separately from each of the road component namely: a) road lot; b) road pavement; c) drainage and slope protection structures; and d) other miscellaneous structures.

We recommended, and Management agreed to instruct the BAC to properly conduct the preliminary examination of bids of bidders outlined in Section 23.1 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 and ensure that bidders rated “passed” submitted the complete eligibility documents such as the legal, technical and financial documents.

We recommended, and Management agreed to instruct the BAC to:
Evaluate each procurement request from different departments to determine the appropriate procurement mode to ensure that correct mode of procurement is conducted.
Refrain from utilizing the Shopping method for procurement transactions if they do not meet the conditions specified in cases a and b of Section 52 in the RIRR of RA No. 9184.
Consider the application of Negotiated procurement through the SVP method under Section 53.9 of RIRR, RA 9184.
Ensure that procurement of goods with ABC amount exceeding P50,000.00 are posted in the PhilGEPS and in three conspicuous places.
Properly evaluate the RFQs/RFPs of prospective suppliers and ensure that it is accompanied by eligibility documents as prescribed in Annex H of the RIRR, RA 9184.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the BAC to:
Implement a rigorous document verification process during post-qualification to validate the authenticity, veracity, and validity of all submitted documents. This process should be conducted using non-discretionary criteria, as outlined in the bidding documents, to ensure fairness and transparency.
Ensure prompt and written notification to the concerned bidder, detailing the grounds for disqualification upon determination of a bidder's failure to meet the post-qualification criteria. This notification should be issued in accordance with Section 34.5 of the RIRR to uphold transparency and accountability.
Refrain from recommending the Head of Procuring Entity for the award of a contract when there has been no proper conduct of the post-qualification process during the procurement of goods. This ensures a fair and competitive bidding process among all other bidders and adherence to the non-negotiable bidding requirements.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:
The BAC to require the winning bidder to post performance security in the prescribed amount and within 10 days (or when Section 37.1 of the RIRR allows a longer period) from the date the NOA was received and prior to entering into contract; and
The Municipal Accountant to require the suppliers or contractors to post warranty security in the amount and form provided under Section 62.1 par. 2 and 62.2.3.3 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 prior to payment, to ensure that risk against manufacturing defects is recovered therefrom. Otherwise, defects and failure within the warranty period not repaired by the suppliers shall be borne by the persons liable.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer:
Improve its communication strategy ensuring that taxpayers receive the necessary notices regarding tax delinquencies. The communication strategy to include clear and concise messages that highlight the importance of paying taxes, the repercussions of delinquency, and the benefits of compliance.
Conduct a tax campaign to inform constituents about the importance of paying taxes, the rationale behind the taxes, and the sanctions related to non-payment. This campaign to be conducted in collaboration with barangay officials to include an explanation of the significance of the Tax Bill. Furthermore, inform the constituents about the benefits, such as discounts, of paying their taxes on a timely basis or in advance to encourage them to comply with their obligations.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Direct the LDRRM Officer to refrain from charging various expenses, such as repairs and maintenance, food supplies, office supplies, travel, fuel, oil, lubricants, and other maintenance and operating expenses, to LDRRM Funds that are not part of the projects and activities listed under item 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.
Provide a separate allocation in the GF for the expenses necessary for the MOOE of the LDRRM Office.

Moving forward, we recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer to determine the unexpended balance of the current year DRRMF and prepare DV to transfer the unexpended current balance of the LDDRMF from GF to TF. Thereafter, the Municipal Treasurer to draw a check and deposit the same to the TF account to effect the actual cash transfer of the unexpended and unobligated balance of the LDRRMF by year-end.

We recommended, and Management agreed to instruct the LDRRM Officer to prepare annually the LDRRMF Investment Plan along with the budget preparation in the prescribed format required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 to ensure that PPAs for disaster preparedness and mitigation are well-planned and implementation ready for such to be timely monitored and evaluated.

14.9 We reiterated our recommendation, and Management agreed to:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare demand letters, duly approved by the Municipal Mayor, for the liquidation of the said cash advances from accountable officers, and monitor the timely submission of liquidation report within the reglementary in accordance with COA Circular No. 97-002.
File request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 and COA Circular 2023-008.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to formulate a GAD Agenda for the remaining term of the LCE in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and be made as basis in the preparation of the annual GPB.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Direct the GFPS to endorse to the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the SB the passing of an ordinance localizing RA 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places; and
Include in the ordinance provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended, and Management agreed to re-examine and update the Local Revenue Code in accordance with the Local Government Code and require the Local Finance Committee to revisit the LGU’s revenue ordinances and amend or revise existing rates considering the current market environment and inflation. Further, update the Revenue Code once every five years as required under Section 191 of the Local Government Code helps to ensure the sustainability and resilience of various local taxes.

We reiterated our recommendation, and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer/GSO to complete the conduct of the annual physical count of PPEs and prepare the PIF to be submitted to both the Audit Team and GSIS for insurance purposes.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management agreed to:
Amend/adjust the RPCPPE/Accounting records for ascertained reconciling PPE items reported in the RPCPPE but not recorded by the Accounting Unit or vice versa to resolve the variance in the total amount of ₱385.327 million;
Report separately the Semi-expendable items costing below 15,000.00; and
Record all PPE items including those found at station in the Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards and to be maintained, henceforth, by the Property Unit and Accounting Unit, respectively.

Management Comment:
The Municipal Accountant and the GSO will convene and agree to resolve the noted variance between the RPCPPE and accounting records. Additionally, both offices will report separately the semi-expandable items below ₱15,000.00.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management concurred to:
Submit immediately the lacking documents required under COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and 2012-001 dated February 12, 2009 and June 14, 2012, respectively;
Establish a master checklist of required documents required under RA 9184, COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and 2012-001 to ensure completeness of the submitted documents.
Submit an explanation for the deficiencies and inconsistencies noted.

Management Comment:
The management is currently on the process of submission to the Commission on Audit (COA) Office of the Audit Team Leader (ATL) all lacking documents and master checklist under COA Circular No 2009-001 and 2012-001. Also, deficiencies and inconsistencies were updated to ensure the completeness of documents for submittal.

Recommendations:
We recommended and for which Management is amenable to:
Empower and direct the designated focal persons and Barangay Captains to specify and initiate projects in accordance with the approved budget and to closely work with the engineering department for assistance in the preparation of the appropriate program of works;
Enforce due dates for the submission of the project proposals after the approval of the budget to fast-track the implementation;
Create effective measures to monitor the project implementation and require close coordination between the BAC and MPDC in the time and manner of procurement, and with the MEO in the execution, providing technical support where necessary; and
Maximize available human resources in each concerned departments, stop the renewal of job order personnel (JOs) who are non-performing and hire instead JOs with needed technical qualifications to best deliver the desired goals and outcomes of the Municipality.

Management Comment:
The management will comply the necessary recommendation to monitor and expedite the implementation of the remaining projects under the 20% Development Fund to adequately deliver the basic services to the constituents and maximize the socio-economic benefits intended to the implementation of feasible socio-economic programs, projects and activities to benefit the general welfare of the constituents in conformity with the provision of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 and RA No. 7160.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management is amenable to:
Appoint an Officer for the MDRRMO with at least a salary grade 22 or 24 to commensurate the heavy workload he performs subject to the availability of funds, to be assisted by three staff responsible for: (1) administration and training; (2) research and planning; and (3) operations and warning, observing the minimum standards and qualifications as specified under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 to properly administer the plans, activities and regular operations of the Office;
Require the MDRRM Office to prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP and the DRRM Research and Planning Staff to prepare and submit promptly the monthly Report on the Utilization of LDRRM Fund following the templates prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 for transparency and proper monitoring of the activities and fund balances of the LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to carefully review such reports; and

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant properly classify the loans payable account of the Municipality into current liabilities if such amounts are due to be settled within 12 months after the reporting date and based on the amortization schedule provided in the loan agreement and into non-current liabilities for the remaining loan amount payable beyond the 12-month period, to provide users of the FSs, reliable and relevant information as far as liquidity and solvency is concerned.

We commended and congratulated the Municipality and its management for having received the awards. We recommend that the Municipality’s officials and employees continuously improve and sustain these local good governance efforts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the members of the Inventory Committee the following:
	
1. To revise the PIP to include all the details as mentioned in the above Circular to ensure that the duties and responsibilities of each member are emphasized that warrant the completion of the physical count of all PPE items.

2. The PIP to include details such as the specific of duties and responsibilities among the committee members, a cut-off date, and a schedule for conducting the inventory taking activities. These data are necessary for the completeness in the conduct of physical count.

3. In the preparation of the RPCPPE to provide complete and accurate descriptions for every PPE items, including their specifications, dimensions, and other pertinent details. This will ensure that the inventory count accurately reflects the nature and identity of the items, which is critical in ascertaining the accuracy and reliability of the balances reflected in the financial statements.

5. We recommended that the LCE to direct the LDRRM Officer and the MDRRMC to monitor the implementation of the LDRRM PPAs to ensure that the Municipality is indeed gearing towards a resilient disaster community.

7. We reiterated our recommendation that the management establish and maintain a GAD database containing sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics in accordance with Item 4 (B) of the above-cited JMC to ensure that PPAs included in the GPB will address emerging and/or continuing issues and concerns such as the provision of basic services and facilities that protect and fulfill women’s human rights, pursue women’s empowerment and gender equality.

We recommended that Management to:

	8. Consider requiring all department heads (i.e., Municipal Health Office and DSWD Office) accountable for the inventories, to conduct physical count of inventories on a semestral basis using the RPCI and submit the same to the MTO for validation and confirmation of the reported stocks on hand.
9. Require all end-users/department heads to maintain stock cards for all stocks including donated goods and regularly update, monitor, balance and reconcile the stock cards against stocks on hand;
10. Require all end-users/departments and MTO to prepare and timely submit to Accounting Office the RIS and RSMI for all inventories issued including donated goods issued during the year.
11. Require Municipal Accountant to effect necessary adjustments to correct inventory balances under trust fund as suggested below:

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:

12. Conduct thorough review, with the involvement of other concerned personnel, and analyze the details of receivables to ascertain their correctness, validity and collectability and send demand letters to persons who have known address to facilitate collections; and

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain the necessary subsidiary ledgers of all revenue collections to establish their accountability at any time and substantiate and reconciled with the GL Balance.

1.We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain complete subsidiary records and financial statements, using the prescribed sub-codes for all the special accounts operated by the LGU and to submit copies submit copies of the same to the Management to provide them of the vital information to effectively manage and implement the different programs/projects and activities of these special accounts.
Such records and financial statements shall also be submitted and made available to this Office to facilitate our review/audit.

2.We also recommended to coordinate with the system provider ECPAC to upgrade the accounting system to include the special accounts.

We recommended that management transfer the correct amount to the trust fund account all unexpended / unobligated balance of the LDRRMF and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE yearly.

We recommended that Management to submit the complete documentations required above to support the payment pursuant to P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We further recommended that Management should enforce strict compliance with guidelines and regulations which require complete documentation before processing any government transaction, otherwise, payments thereof will be suspended or disallowed.

Also, liquidation reports for cash advances should always be substantiated with Official Receipts or proof of payments.

We recommended that Management require the designated Municipal Accountant to ensure that submission of the required monthly reports be on time as prescribed under Section 122 of P.D. 1445, COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We also recommended to the management to immediately hire a CPA to occupy the Municipal Accountant, a mandatory position of the LGU.

We recommended that management instruct the concerned personnel/unit to:

a.Fast track the disposal of the obsolete unused checks in compliance with the procedures provided in the above-mentioned laws, rules and regulations; and

b) Ensure thereafter that all unserviceable/obsolete properties/items be properly accounted, documented and disposed accordingly.

We recommended that the Management review the proper recording of the Investments in Joint Venture account in the books. Specifically, the Municipal Accountant:

a. 	Request for the latest financial statements of the JV from PANTELCO, and on that basis, prepare the appropriate journal entry to reflect the correct balance of the Investments in Joint Venture account as at December 31, 2020 in the Municipality’s books.


b. 	Thereafter, applying the equity method prescribed in IPSAS 36, record annually any income/loss from the JV operation in the Municipality’s books using the proper revenue or expense accounts and an adjustment to the Investments in Joint Venture account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant include in the Notes to FSs the required disclosures on the reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the SCBAA and in the SFPerf for the year then ended. Its format be based on Annex 10 of the GAM for LGUs Volume I which was prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-004 and published on August 28, 2020.

We recommended that Management should exert more efforts to locate the supporting documents of this loan receivable account. These documents will provide information to support the claims of the LGU to demand payments. And should said efforts fail, the LGU shall request COA for the condonation/write off of the receivables accounts subject to prescribed guidelines.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the drivers and other concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Audit Team every month for audit examination and evaluation.

We recommended that Management require:
The Municipal Treasurer to:
Ensure preparation of the Schedule of Unreleased Checks, a list of checks which are unreleased or undelivered at the end of the year, and submission thereof to the Municipal Accountant;
Use the prescribed form of the Check Register and ensure that the required fields/details are properly filled in.
The Municipal Accountant to refer to the Schedule of Unreleased Checks for the preparation of the adjusting entry to restore the value of the unreleased checks to the cash account balance and set-up the appropriate liability, and make an entry the following year to reverse such adjustments.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Exert more effort to locate pertinent supporting documents and prepare subsidiary ledgers for the receivable accounts based thereon;
Assess/evaluate the condition of all dormant receivables in order to determine whether which are still recoverable and/or which are subject to possible write-off.
For receivables determined to be recoverable, exhaust all possible remedies to enforce collection thereof.  Likewise, issue demand letters to the identified NGAs and NGOs/POs to immediately liquidate the funds transferred to them.
For receivables determined to be beyond recovery in consonance with COA Circular No. 2016-005, facilitate the filing of request for authority to write-off following the guidelines and procedures set forth in the Circular.  Also, recognize impairment for accounts no longer collectible pursuant to the aforementioned accounting standards.
Ensure documentation, regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure prompt collection thereof when these become due and demandable, liquidation of fund transfers within the prescribed period depending on their nature and purpose, and to prevent further accumulation of dormant accounts.

We recommended that Management:
a. 	Direct the Inventory Committee/ Appraisal Committee to fast track the completion of the conduct of physical count of all PPEs / supplies, both serviceable and unserviceable, prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and submit the same to the Accounting Unit to serve as basis in the recording of Impairment Loss; and
b. 	Create and require the Disposal Committee to refer to the procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 89-296 for the disposal of properties.

We recommended that Management:
Fast track the following:
Retrieval and submission of the copies of the tax declaration, deed of sale and/or deed of donation to COA, and ensure thereafter that the original copies thereof are properly secured and stored for easy retrieval;
Conduct of inventory and appraisal of all the lands owned by the Municipality wherein with regards to the latter, Management can opt to have an in-house appraisal or hire a duly qualified appraiser subject to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its implementing rules and regulations;
Upon retrieval of the documents in 1.a above, instruct:
The Municipal Accountant to:
Update the balance of the “Land” account by taking up the material difference between the book value of the land presented in the FS and the market values per the Municipal Assessor’s records amounting to ₱48.542 million using the following entry:
Land        ₱48.542M
  PPA           ₱48.542M
Prepare/update the ledger cards and ledgers, and prepare/maintain corresponding subsidiary ledgers thereof;
The GSO to update the property cards for the Land account.
Based on the appraisal and inventory reports in 1.b above, require:
The Municipal Accountant to update the ledger cards, ledgers, and subsidiary ledgers of the “Land” account and prepare necessary adjustments for any discrepancy between the updated “Land” account balance in 2.a above and the new appraised values of the properties;
The GSO to update the property cards for any discrepancy between the records in #2 above and the appraisal/inventory reports.
Include in its accounting policy and budget provision the regular appraisal and revaluation of the PPE accounts, the frequency of which is left to Management’s discretion and must be based on historical data or available market data/studies.

We recommended that Management:
In the absence of transfer documents from the donor, initiate the appraisal of the donated disaster and rescue equipment and materials in order to come up with the appropriate value/s to be taken up in the books;
Based on the results of the appraisal in #1 above:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the fair market value/appraised value of the equipment and materials into the appropriate asset accounts, bearing in mind the PPE capitalization threshold requirement of ₱15,000.00 as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
Instruct the General Services Officer to update the ARE by indicating the corresponding fair market value/appraised value of each property.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to appropriately recognize Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits in the books;
Direct the Budget Officer to include the subject payables in budgeting considerations giving priority to the current year amounts should management find it difficult to immediately fund the whole amount of payables; and
With the current ease of COVID-19 restrictions, maximize the revenue-raising power of the municipality to identify additional source of revenue to fund the whole amount of leave benefits payable.

We recommended that management instruct the concerned AOs to strictly take into account the serial numbers of cash tickets upon receipt and issuance thereof, and ensure that issuance of AF-55 is in strict numerical sequence as provided in the aforementioned manuals.

We recommended that Management direct the GAD focal person to ensure that programs/projects/activities formulated and included in the GPB and subsequently undertaken and charged against the GAD budget are in line with the purpose for which GAD was created which, above all, should address gender issues in adherence to the requirements of PCW-NEDA-DILG JMC No. 2016-01.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

   a. The Municipality’s Inventory Committee  conduct  a  complete  physical
      count of all the properties at least  once  a  year  and  prepare  the
      required reports thereof.


   b. The Property Officer and the Municipal  Accountant  maintain  property
      cards  and  property  ledger  cards,  respectively,  and  update  them
      periodically. Thereafter, cause the inventory reports to be reconciled
      with the reported balances of PPE accounts to  establish  the  correct
      balances presented in the financial statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management  review  the  proper
   recording of the Investments in  Joint  Venture  account  in  the  books.
   Specifically, the Municipal Accountant:

   a. Request the latest financial statements of the JV from  PANTELCO,  and
      on that basis, prepare the appropriate journal entry  to  reflect  the
      correct balance of the Investments in  Joint  Venture  account  as  at
      December 31, 2021 in the Municipality’s books.


   b. Thereafter, applying the equity method prescribed in IPSAS 36,  record
      annually any income/loss from the JV operation in  the  Municipality’s
      books using the proper revenue or expense accounts and  an  adjustment
      to the Investments in Joint Venture account.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor and all other Municipal officials and
   employees strictly comply with the requirements and mechanics  prescribed
   under Presidential EO No.  77  in  their  claims  for  reimbursement  for
   official travels. Specifically:

   a. Official local travels of the Municipal Mayor  be  covered  with  duly
      approved travel orders/authorities to travel issued by the  Provincial
      Governor.

   b. All claims for official travel in accordance with the rates authorized
      under Presidential EO No. 77.

   c. Accommodation claims in excess of the accommodation component  of  the
      DTE, but not to exceed 100% of such component,  be  supported  with  a
      certification by the Head of  the  Agency  that  those  expenses  were
      absolutely necessary in the performance of their assignments.

   d. Expenses of the Municipal Mayor other than travelling expenses, during
      the duration of the travel, be  charged  to  other  line-item  expense
      budgets, subject to availability  of  funds  and  the  limits  set  in
      Section 325 (h) of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160.

We recommended the following:

   a. The Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Treasurer immediately return the
      unutilized balance of funds erroneously transferred from  the  General
      Fund to the Trust Fund. These amounts shall be treated as surplus  and
      shall only be utilized upon subsequent enactment of  an  appropriation
      ordinance.


   b. The Municipal Accountant prepare  the  necessary  journal  entries  to
      correct  and/or  adjust  the  proper  recording   of   the   financial
      transactions in the books as a result of the erroneous  transfers  and
      the recommended subsequent reversions.


   c. Management strictly adhere to the provisions of the LGC on trust funds
      and reversion of unexpended balances of appropriations  and  instantly
      stop the practice of transferring unutilized/expiring funds  from  the
      General Fund to the Trust Fund.

We recommended that the BAC provide complete documentation  and  strictly
   comply with the requirements of Annex “G” of the 2016 Revised IRR  of  RA
   No.  9184  in  the  procurement  and  implementation  of   infrastructure
   contracts under the Design and Build scheme.

We recommended the following:

   a.  The  Municipal  Engineer  and  the  Municipal  Mayor  issue  only   a
      Certificate of Final Acceptance on a construction contract  after  one
      year from project completion  to  maximize  and  extend  the  warranty
      provided by the Performance Security up to  the  end  of  the  defects
      liability period.


   b. Management require contractors to  submit  Warranty  Security  in  the
      required form after issuance of the Certificate of Final Acceptance.

We recommended that the BAC see to it that, in the purchase of equipment,
   requests for quotations should not  contain  references  to  brand  names
   and/or brand models and strictly comply with Section 18  of  the  Revised
   IRR of RA No. 9184 that they only mention the  relevant  characteristics,
   functionality and/or performance requirements of the equipment needed.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant include in
   the Notes to FSs the required disclosures on the  reconciliation  between
   actual amounts on a comparable basis as presented in the SCBAA and in the
   SFPerf for the year then ended. Its format be based on  Annex  G  of  COA
   Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We recommended the following:

   a. The Municipal Accountant separately prepare disbursement vouchers  for
      the payment of  salaries,  RATA  and  wages  of  job  order  personnel
      attaching  the  payrolls  and  obligation   requests   pertaining   to
      legislative, all to be signed by the Municipal Vice-Mayor.


   b. The Municipal Vice-Mayor countersign all  checks  issued  out  of  the
      appropriations of the Office of the Vice-Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan  and
      the Secretary to the Sangguniang Bayan. During the Vice-Mayor’s  leave
      of absence, the OIC of the Office  of  the  Vice-Mayor  take  over  as
      counter-signatory of checks.

Prospectively, we recommended that all municipal  cashiers  refrain  from
   accepting similar positions which involve the handling of cash  from  any
   other association or organization.

We commended and congratulated the Municipality and  its  management  for
   having received  the  awards.  We  recommended  that  the  Municipality’s
   officials and employees continuously improve and sustain these local good
   governance efforts.

b. 	The Municipal Accountant record expenses to their appropriate accounts considering the nature or purpose of the transactions in consonance with what is prescribed in the Chart of Accounts embodied in Volume III of the GAM for LGUs.

We recommended the following:

a. 	Management explain in writing why the water system projects constructed in CY 2013 and CY 2014 with total project cost of ₱2.464 million was abandoned and partially demolished/disposed without the inspection and appraisal of the COA Auditor.

b. 	The Municipal Engineer prepare and submit a report to account for the materials extracted from the abandoned and partially demolished/disposed water system projects such as quantity and type of materials remained buried/unremoved, quantity and type of materials distributed for re-use, quantity and type of materials damaged and safekept, or materials lost, if any. The report be reconciled with the quantity and type of materials used in the implementation of the said water system projects as shown in their Programs of Work.

c. 	The Municipal Accountant obtain disposal documents from concerned officers such as the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), Municipal Engineer’s Report, Disposal Committee Report, Demolition Permit and Sangguniang Bayan Resolution and properly record the disposal of the unserviceable water system projects in the books.

d. 	Prospectively, all properties of the Municipality to be disposed be inspected and appraised by the COA Auditor first in compliance with RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant facilitate the submission of the cash advance voucher for CF and all its supporting documents to the COA ATL within seven (7) days after release of check for timely evaluation and audit.

We recommended that all accounting entries for all cash and non-cash transactions be properly signed “prepared by” by the person in charge and “certified correct” by the Municipal Accountant before recording the journal entries of the transactions in the books and before submission to the Office of the Auditor.

We commended and congratulated the Municipality and its management for having received the awards. We recommend that the Municipality’s officials and employees continuously improve and sustain these good local governance efforts.

We recommended that:

a. 	The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator set up and maintain a GAD database or incorporate it to its existing IComSys database which should contain complete data requirements enumerated in Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. 

b. 	Management provide budgetary and technical support to the MPDO in the setting up and maintenance of a GAD database.

c. 	The GAD Focal Point System – Technical Working Group use sex-disaggregated data and other GAD-related information provided by the GAD database in identifying gender issues and gaps and their related projects and activities for inclusion in the gender-responsive GPB.

We recommended that:

Municipal Treasurer send a written notice to the payees or holders in due course to surrender the stale checks for cancellation and issuance of a new one.

Municipal Accountant make necessary adjusting entry for stale checks that remained outstanding and cannot be presented for payment to determine the correct balance of Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account and Accounts Payable account.

We recommended that management comply with the reporting requirements on fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. The LDRRMO should prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and the same shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LCD) respectively.

We recommended that the Management document and submit to the Commission on Audit the request for authority to write-off the account balances which were considered dormant and could no longer be collected pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
