Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Issue new demand letters to concerned officers and employees as a formal demand to liquidate their cash advances.
If upon demand, the concerned officials and officers still fail to liquidate their cash advances, immediately deduct the unliquidated amount from their salaries.
Conduct a regular review, reconciliation and monitoring of all booked receivables to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable and that cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed period depending on their nature and purpose.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Disbursing Officer and the Accountant observe strictly the documentary requirements in the granting and liquidation of cash advances embodied under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and COA Circular No. 97-002 and ensure that payment of salaries is duly acknowledged by recipients on the payroll. We also recommend that the Disbursing Officer assign reference numbers to the report of disbursements for easier tracking.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Exert diligent efforts to locate the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents and to prepare subsidiary ledgers of the receivable accounts.
Issue demand letters to the NGAs and NGOs/POs to immediately liquidate the funds transferred to them in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013.
Conduct a regular review, reconciliation and monitoring of all booked receivables to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable and that the fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending on their nature and purpose.
Analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and recognize impairment for accounts no longer collectible.
If collections of these accounts are no longer probable, prepare the necessary documents and submit request for write-off to COA pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the required subsidiary ledgers for the Trust Liabilities – DRRM by year of transfer.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Accountant to:
Review all the subsidiary ledger balances maintained against the general ledger balances;
Determine causes of the difference; and
Request assistance to service provider for the correction/updating of ledgers with discrepancies.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the following:
Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to ensure that disbursements related to LDRRM are properly charged to the appropriate funds and in accordance to the LDRRM Investment Plans; and to submit the prescribed annual reports on the utilization of DRRM Funds; and
Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents to sufficiently support the disbursements from the Quick Response Fund.

Recommendation:
In order to comply with the provisions of RA 9003, we recommended that Management allocate funds for this purpose and:
Develop the Municipal’s Solid Waste Management Plan and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Board for review and approval
Cease the operation of the open dump site immediately and hasten the completion of the required legal documents to prepare the propose site for sanitary landfill
Apply/ obtain an Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC) from the DENR for the propose operation of the sanitary landfill in Barangay Gimaylan and refer to Rule XIV in IRR of RA 9003 for the minimum considerations in operating sanitary landfills.
Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to closely monitor the compliance and implementation of the provisions under RA 9003

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the following:
Municipal Accountant-Designate to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 20th day after the end of each month as prescribed under Section 70 of the NGAS Manual;
Likewise, turn over the receipts and disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transaction to the Auditor within 10 days of the ensuing month in compliance with Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

Recommendation
We recommended that Management review the amount of cash accountability granted to the Disbursing Officer during the previous payroll periods and increase the amount of bond based on Annex C (Revised Schedule of Premium Rates) of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management make representations to the Sangguniang Bayan to expedite the approval of the Local Ordinance for the operation of the Community Fish Landing Center and post-harvest facility at Gimaylan that will generate additional revenue for the LGU as well as maximize the profits of the fisherfolk.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal DRRM Officer to ensure that the planned disaster-related activities and programs are implemented within the year.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers and prepare and submit the post-closing trial balance and financial statements and reports of the local economic enterprises to the auditor and to the Local Chief Executive and to the Sanggunian.

We recommended that the BAC procure POL of the Municipality strictly in accordance with RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR. Specifically, we recommend the following:


1.	Procure POL through competitive bidding process. Resort to alternative methods of procurement only when justified by circumstances specifically provided in RA No. 9184, its revised IRR, and GPPB issuances;

2.	Discontinue reimbursement as mode of payment for POL; and

3.	Include POL in the offices’ PPMPs and the consolidated APP.

We recommended that the Municipal School Board formulate the school board budget strictly in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 7160 and its implementing rules in the utilization of the SEF. Only items among the required and priority projects enumerated in the regulations be allowed to be funded out of the SEF. Responsible officials should cease and desist charging expenditures of the office of the district supervisor from the school board budget.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the reporting requirements on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. In particular, the Municipal Accountant should timely prepare the required monthly report and the same shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC).

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRM presently recorded in the General Fund to a Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund Books in order to ensure continued and exclusive use of the fund for disaster-risk reduction management activities within the next five years. The accounting entries are shown in COA Circular No. 2012-002 for proper guidance.

We recommended that Management strictly submit the cash advance vouchers for CF and all its supporting documents to the COA ATL within seven (7) days after release of checks for timely evaluation and audit. We recommended that management submit the duly filled up Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation Form on the audit observations and recommendations embodied in the CY 2015 and the ensuing years AARs to the Office of the Auditor. We recommended that in the absence of government vehicles, actual fare or reasonable fare be claimed on reimbursement basis in incurrence of official travels. We recommended that the management refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advances are liquidated. Cash advances shall be fully liquidated and settled at year-end so that proper adjustments could be made on both the particular expense account and the asset account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management should direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the amount withheld to the BIR pursuant to RA 8291, PD 1752 and RR No. 2-98 so that the same could be utilized by the recipient agency for the benefit of their constituents/members and to avoid penalties due to delayed remittance.

We recommended to require the following:
That the Municipal Mayor coordinate with the Assessor’s Office to enhance collection efficiency and take initiatives to find ways. Use the assessment roll in setting up the targeted receivables-Real Property Tax to arrive at a more realistic and reliable amount, thereby avoiding a material amount of receivable which could be doubtful as to collectability.  
Adapt strategic means or method to improve the collection efficiency through the conduct of information drive among its residents so that they will be aware of the necessity and advantage in making municipal government financially capable.
observations.reason: The Management has exerted effort to intensify tax collection.
